name: OpenClaw
summary: OpenClaw missing authentication for local browser-control endpoints
description:
Summary

The local browser-control HTTP interface allowed sensitive browser actions without mandatory authentication in some configurations. This could allow unauthorized local callers to invoke privileged browser-control operations against managed sessions.

Affected Components

- src/browser/routes/agent.act.ts
- src/browser/pw-tools-core.interactions.ts
- Browser control HTTP route: /agent/act

Affected Versions

- >= 2026.1.5, <= 2026.2.9

Patched Versions

- >= 2026.2.12

Technical Details (sanitized)

The browser-control route accepted high-privilege action requests that can execute browser-context logic. Authentication was not consistently enforced for these HTTP routes when auth was not configured. Given the route is loopback-bound, exposure is primarily local-process and local-proxy boundary related.

Impact

Potential unauthorized access to managed browser session capabilities, including access to sensitive in-session data and privileged browser actions.

Remediation

- Enforce gateway auth on browser-control HTTP routes (token/password).
- Auto-generate and persist a strong gateway.auth.token when browser control is enabled without existing auth.
- Add security-audit detection for browser control without auth.
- Add regression tests for auth enforcement and audit coverage.

Credit

Thanks @tcusolle for the responsible report.
suggestion: Upgrade openclaw to >= 2026.2.12 or later. Commit(s) - 9230a2ae14307740a13ada7afd6dcfab34e0287f
